caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The deterministic 4-digit 5614 role mix includes sibling-code occupations and is not a 561422 staffing census.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 0.4171 residual wage share is an unobserved mix judgment, not a measured 561422 role share.
inputs.pi_dist: Large-provider and platform disclosures are proxies for small domestic bureau contracts.
inputs.pi_moat: No target-specific client concentration, contract-transfer or certification evidence was available.
inputs.t50_years: This is an effective-work adoption estimate, not a measured 561422 adoption survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Do not interpret as the share of companies with fully autonomous contact handling.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is a causal analogy, not a measured historical elasticity.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No observed 561422 ownership-age series was located.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not infer a succession shortage from general small-business demographics.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a verified count of active buyers for $1-10M EBITDA 561422 targets.
inputs.buy_mult: The disclosed Majorel precedent is not NAICS 561422, not US lower-middle-market and includes strategic scale/synergy value.
inputs.exit_mult: There is no disclosed small 561422 exit transaction multiple; this is a risk-adjusted underwriting judgment.
inputs.pricing_structure: No target contract sample was reviewed; RingCentral is a technology-vendor proxy rather than an outsourced-BPO operator.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[56]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $84,840 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 561422); class <5: 926 firms, avg revenue/firm $563,971 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $88,261 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 247 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,328,636 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $207,931 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 210 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,113,986 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $487,339 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 400 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,157,090 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $807,085 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 201 firms, avg revenue/firm $24,411,512 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,820,402 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 315 firms, avg revenue/firm $77,024,206 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,054,288 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 201

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Routine customer-contact, telemarketing, collection, QA and coordination tasks are substantially exposed, while escalations, complex cases and client-specific judgment remain. The 4-digit occupational proxy is imperfect, so the aggregate is bounded rather than presented as observed staffing data.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 561422 ($17,861,129,852) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 561422 ($32,736,522,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact-artifact identity, source coverage and arithmetic pass. All four score-driving sources are accessible and support the directional thesis or disclosed calculation. The record is publishable with its authored proxy, scope and uncertainty caveats; no evidence defect, reversed semantics or capture/terminal double count is likely to change the factors, zero aggregate score, kill verdict or melting-base thesis.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 561422 Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers
sources.what_it_supports: Exact NAICS scope: third-party contact centers initiate or receive communications for clients and do not own the represented product or service.

sources.title: Concentrix Corporation 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: A scaled CX provider identifies AI and self-service as able to replace or supplement human contacts and warns of pricing-model and available-market pressure.

sources.title: Teleperformance amendment to 2022 universal registration document: Majorel acquisition
sources.what_it_supports: Disclosed Majorel acquisition consideration and 2022 operating EBITDA used only as a large, international strategic valuation reference, with an explicit small-target discount.

sources.title: RingCentral 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Contact-center technology vendor disclosure that AI-led products are offered on a usage-based pricing model; this is a platform proxy, not BPO-operator pricing data.

title: Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers
